Patch Set 10:

> Looks like a sanity-sec failure, may be related to this patch?
 > 
 > https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/7a83ca92-d314-11e8-82f2-52540065bddc

I am investigating the issue, it looks like the request is actually modified after checksum is computed on client side.

On MDS:

  Oct 18 19:24:21 trevis-54vm4 kernel: LustreError: 20317:0:(gss_sk_mech.c:390:sk_verify_hmac()) checksum mismatch
  Oct 18 19:24:21 trevis-54vm4 kernel: LustreError: 20317:0:(sec_gss.c:242:gss_verify_msg()) mic verify error: 00060000
  Oct 18 19:24:21 trevis-54vm4 kernel: LustreError: 20317:0:(sec_gss.c:2122:gss_svc_verify_request()) failed to verify request: 60000
  Oct 18 19:24:21 trevis-54vm4 kernel: LustreError: 20317:0:(sec_gss.c:2285:gss_svc_handle_data()) svc 2 failed: major 0x00060000: req xid 1614692387651856 ctx ffff953f680a9a40 idx 0x93bbf0d0e5ad3450(0->10.9.6.100@tcp)

It seems to be related to the fact that the client and servers are configured with an additional @tcp999 NID, and we want to force use of this one.

On MDS side, we can see that the request is received from NID client@tcp0 instead of client@tcp999, for instance with this message:

  Oct 18 19:24:21 trevis-54vm4 lsvcgssd[13483]: handling req: svc 1, nid 000200000a090664, idx 93bbf0d0e5ad3450 nodemap default

NID 000200000a090664 is on tcp0, it should be 000203e70a090664 on tcp999.
